comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
EVIDENCE for the repository-gate ACs (make lint / make typecheck / make test) — these are execution-based and the completion verifier cannot run commands, so recording the proof here:

1) Gerrit change 752 (this ticket's change, https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/752) has Verified+1 from the CI bot. CI runs 'make lint' (ruff check + format-check + zizmor + actionlint), 'make typecheck' (mypy over src/rebar), and the full test suite (unit + integration tiers, py3.11/3.12/3.13 on ubuntu + macos) on GitHub Actions, and only votes Verified+1 when ALL pass. Winning run: https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/29553885935 (SUCCESS).

2) Local pre-flight from this worktree (branch jira-reb-1003-portability, on origin/main cb0f8ec3b + stack head 93f593b4f):
   - make lint -> 'All checks passed!' (ruff) + 'No findings to report' (zizmor) + actionlint clean.
   - make typecheck -> 'Success: no issues found in 327 source files'.
   - full CI-equivalent suite (pytest -m 'not integration and not external' -n 3 --dist worksteal) -> 6303 passed, 18 skipped; the only 3 failures are machine-environmental (test_config_perf/test_config_show, caused by this host having a ~/.config/rebar user config; absent in CI's clean env, where they pass).
   - the built-in move-table parity test (test_move_registry_matches_docs_table) is GREEN with the new dogfood subsection (placed outside the parsed numeric-id table).

Together the Verified+1 CI vote and the local runs demonstrate make lint, make typecheck, and make test pass.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The project-specific criterion and coaching move are intended to be a concrete client example of the supported extension boundary. Documentation must show the composition accurately without making project moves appear to be packaged built-ins.

## What

Document the rebar portability guard in a separate project-extension subsection, add a structural documentation test, and run the complete repository regression gates after all implementation children land.

## Scope

In scope:

- `docs/plan-review-gate.md`
- `tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py`
- final repository lint, typecheck, and test verification

Out of scope:

- changing criterion, move, verifier, or disposition behavior
- documenting the project move inside the parsed built-in move table
- remediating pre-existing portability debt

## Approach

First update the existing `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph so its schema reads `{move_id: {name, template, applies_when?}}` and states that absent or empty `applies_when` is always applicable while a non-empty list intersects active criterion triggers. Then add `### Dogfooding a project portability guard` immediately after that paragraph and before `### The advisory cap`. The structural placement anchor is the end of the existing sentence `never hand over a solution.`; this location is deliberately outside the built-in move table parsed by `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py::test_move_registry_matches_docs_table`.

The subsection is the client-facing composition example and contains:

- the exact `project.portability` `plan_review` routing entry with `exec: 1-TURN`, `facet: project-invariants`, `applies_at.scope: [container, leaf]`, `default_posture: blocking`, `block_threshold: 0.9`, plus `activate: [project.portability]`;
- the prompt path `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`, `execution_mode: single_turn`, the four exact rubric headings, plus the typed field mapping from `immune-floury-toad`;
- the four supported shape dimensions with all values specified by the finder child;
- the exact rules `Silence about portability is not a finding` plus `Project-specific behavior behind project configuration or an explicit extension boundary is allowed`;
- the exact `project-portability` move id, name, `applies_when`, plus locked template from `truthful-specified-silverfish`;
- the balanced eight-case eval path with exact release thresholds `recall: 1.0`, `false_accept: 0.0`, `agreement: 1.0`, per-case `stability >= 0.6666666667`, plus expected-vs-observed fire/no-fire `kappa >= 0.70`;
- the responsibility split: Pass 1 discovers a cited concrete counterexample, unchanged Pass 2 verifies validity/impact, unchanged Pass 3 decides disposition, Pass 4 consumes the project move for surviving findings.

This one subsection is the durable dogfood example requested by the epic; it does not restate generic architecture in a new ADR. The structural test reads the real document, locates the subsection by its exact heading, verifies all literals, then proves its byte range begins after the end of `never hand over a solution.` and ends before `### The advisory cap`.

## Grounding

`docs/plan-review-gate.md` already documents the built-in Pass-4 table at `### The Pass-4 move registry` plus the project extension paragraph immediately after it. `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py::test_move_registry_matches_docs_table` extracts only numeric-id table rows and requires exact parity with packaged `MOVE_REGISTRY`; placing a hyphenated project id in a separate subsection preserves that parser contract. The parent experiment explicitly confirmed this placement decision.

## Dependencies

Depends on the criterion, coaching, and calibration children so the documentation describes the final validated behavior rather than an intermediate design.

## Test Strategy

Use a focused structural documentation test for required examples and placement, then run all repository gates from the fresh worktree.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The existing project-extension paragraph documents `{move_id: {name, template, applies_when?}}`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k project_move_schema`.
- [ ] The existing project-extension paragraph defines absent, empty, plus non-empty `applies_when` behavior. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k project_move_schema`.
- [ ] The guide contains the exact heading `### Dogfooding a project portability guard`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k subsection_heading`.
- [ ] The subsection contains the exact `project.portability` routing entry plus activation list. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k routing_example`.
- [ ] The subsection names the project prompt path plus its exact execution mode. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k prompt_contract`.
- [ ] The subsection contains the four rubric headings plus the typed finding-field mapping. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k finding_contract`.
- [ ] The subsection contains every supported client-shape label-value pair. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k client_shape_matrix`.
- [ ] The subsection contains both exact non-finding rules. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k false_positive_boundaries`.
- [ ] The subsection contains the exact project move id, name, trigger, plus template. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k coaching_example`.
- [ ] The subsection contains all five exact live calibration thresholds. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k calibration_example`.
- [ ] The subsection states each pass responsibility specified in this plan. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k pass_separation`.
- [ ] The project subsection begins after `never hand over a solution.` plus ends before `### The advisory cap`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k parser_safe_placement`.
- [ ] The existing built-in move-table parity test remains green. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review.py -k move_registry_matches_docs_table`.
- [ ] Repository lint passes. Prove with `make lint`.
- [ ] Repository type checks pass. Prove with `make typecheck`.
- [ ] The complete repository test suite passes. Prove with `make test`.
